name: Neutrinoless double-beta decay: Majorana effective mass
requirement: Any candidate framework predicting Majorana neutrinos must respect upper bounds on the effective Majorana mass <m_betabeta> from neutrinoless double-beta-decay searches. KamLAND-Zen 2023 (Xe-136) sets <m_betabeta> < (28-122) meV at 90% CL, depending on which nuclear matrix elements are used. Detection of 0nubb would prove neutrinos are Majorana fermions and probe lepton-number violation at the TeV scale.

regime.description: Nuclear-scale lepton-number-violating process (Delta L = 2). Q-values at the MeV scale; sensitivity to TeV-scale BSM through the standard light-Majorana-exchange mechanism or directly via heavy mediators.

current_bound.value: <m_betabeta> < 28-122 meV (Xe-136); < 79-180 meV (Ge-76, GERDA + LEGEND-200); < 36-156 meV (Te-130, CUORE)
current_bound.units: meV (90% CL upper limit on effective Majorana mass)
current_bound.reference: Abe et al. (KamLAND-Zen), 'Search for the Majorana Nature of Neutrinos in the Inverted Mass Ordering Region with KamLAND-Zen', PRL 130, 051801 (2023), arXiv:2203.02139; Agostini et al. (GERDA), PRL 125, 252502 (2020); Adams et al. (CUORE), Nature 604, 53 (2022)

what_it_excludes: If neutrinos are inverted-ordering Majorana with the lightest mass below ~10 meV, the predicted <m_betabeta> band is ~15-50 meV - approaching the most aggressive KamLAND-Zen bound. nEXO, LEGEND-1000, CUPID aim to probe the entire inverted-ordering region within the next decade. Discovery would establish lepton-number violation; non-detection over the inverted band would favor normal ordering or Dirac neutrinos.

parameterization.name: Effective Majorana mass <m_betabeta> = |sum_i U_ei^2 m_i| (with complex Majorana phases). For BSM heavy-particle exchange, parameterized by SMEFT operators of dimension >= 7 violating lepton number.
parameterization.reference: Bilenky and Petcov, RMP 59, 671 (1987); Cirigliano et al., 'Neutrinoless Double-Beta Decay: A Roadmap for Matching Theory to Experiment', arXiv:2203.12169 (2022).
parameterization.summary: Decay rate Gamma_0nubb proportional to G_0nu * |M_0nu|^2 * <m_betabeta>^2 / m_e^2, where G_0nu is a phase-space factor and M_0nu the nuclear matrix element.

procedural.predicted_observable: Half-life T^0nu_(1/2) of a 0nubb-candidate isotope, converted to <m_betabeta> via T^0nu_(1/2) = (G_0nu |M_0nu|^2 <m_betabeta>^2 / m_e^2)^(-1).
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: <m_betabeta> < 28 meV (best Xe-136 bound assuming favorable NME).
framework_inputs: If the framework predicts Majorana neutrinos, compute <m_betabeta> from the mass spectrum and PMNS matrix.
If the framework includes heavy LNV mediators, match onto Delta L = 2 SMEFT operators and compute their contribution to the 0nubb rate.
Nuclear matrix elements |M_0nu| have ~factor-2 uncertainties; report bounds bracketing the NME range.

primary_references.citation: Abe et al. (KamLAND-Zen Collaboration), 'Search for the Majorana Nature of Neutrinos in the Inverted Mass Ordering Region with KamLAND-Zen', PRL 130, 051801 (2023)
Cirigliano et al., 'Neutrinoless Double-Beta Decay: A Roadmap for Matching Theory to Experiment' (2022)
